execution_summary.quality_notes: Dokumentasjonsgrunnlaget for de sju kliniske funnene er basert på to systematiske oversikter (nivå 2) og én enkeltstudie (nivå 1). Det er observert samsvar i funnene på tvers av de inkluderte kildene. Dokumentasjonsstyrken er primært forankret i systematiske oversikter, noe som gir et metodisk utgangspunkt for anbefalingene, selv om det totale antallet kilder er begrenset.

quotes: Conclusion: Daytime sleepiness was the most common clinical symptom motivating home mechanical ventilation in this group of patients with chronic hypercapnic respiratory insufficiency secondary to neuro/myopathies.

E-mail: ml@laub.dk Submitted April 21, 2005; accepted March 17, 2006 INTRODUCTION Home mechanical ventilation (HMV) in patients with chronic alveolar hypoventilation secondary to neuromuscular diseases (NMD) has become widely acceptable, as the provision of ventilatory support can provide symptomatic relief and increase life expectancy (1).
HMV can be applied as non-invasive ventilation (NIV) most often via a facemask, or as invasive ventilation via a tracheo- stomy.

Only patients starting after this date and presenting neuromuscular diseases as the cause of respiratory failure (n/352) were included in our analysis, which was comprised of the following diagnostic groups: (a) post-polio syndrome, (b) amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), (c) Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD), (d) dystrophia myotonica (DM), (e) spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and (f) other neuro/myopathies.

nursing_diagnosis.term: risiko for nedsett funksjon i respirasjonssystem

quotes: Only patients starting after this date and presenting neuromuscular diseases as the cause of respiratory failure (n/352) were included in our analysis, which was comprised of the following diagnostic groups: (a) post-polio syndrome, (b) amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), (c) Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD), (d) dystrophia myotonica (DM), (e) spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and (f) other neuro/myopathies.
Opposing this, acute patients presented a rapid worsening in their condition, often induced by an infection, leading to severe respiratory insufficiency requiring immediate medical attention.
We wish to point out that the ALS group fits well into the general pattern, with respect to acute vs elective launching of HMV, as well as to motives and physiological data.

We call attention to this since there is a special focus on patients with ALS regarding not only the rapid aggravation in the disease but the ethical aspects on HMV, especially invasive ventilation, as well.
Patients with ALS usually succumb to respiratory complications, on average, within 5 years of symptom onset ( 1 , 4 – 7 ).

Interestingly, only the progression of respiratory dysfunction, as measured by the ALSFORD-R-Respiratory score (effect size: −0.81), was significantly less decreased in the exercise group as compared to the control group.
Accordingly, early initiation of therapeutic exercise would have been performed primarily for respiratory muscles rather than limb muscles.

It has already been reported that the early initiation of exercise in patients with ALS is more effective than starting exercise at later stages of the disease ( 20 ), therefore therapeutic exercise would be more effective for maintaining respiratory function than limb motor function in patients with ALS.

goal.term: i stand til å utføre daglige rutiner

quotes: Muscle weakness is considered one of the major symptoms of ALS.
Initial muscle weakness usually occurs in isolated muscles, most often distally, and is followed by rapidly progressive weakness and functional limitations.
Therefore, patients with ALS must maintain activities of daily living as much as possible with symptomatic treatments such as therapeutic exercises ( 13 , 14 ).
These exercises consist of strengthening, resistive, or active aerobic exercises ( 13 , 14 ).
In any case, the marked reduction in physical activity that often accompanies ALS can lead to cardiovascular deconditioning and muscle weakness secondary to disuse ( 18 ).
Reduced physical activity, particularly if prolonged, also produces muscle atrophy, osteoporosis, and reduced strength of tendons and ligaments.

Appropriate exercises may be physically and psychologically beneficial to patients with ALS, especially in the earlier stages of the disease and before significant muscular atrophy or deconditioning occurs.

Although exercise may not improve the strength of muscles already weakened by ALS, strengthening exercises with low to moderate weights, and aerobic exercises such as walking, swimming, and bicycling at submaximal levels (i.e., therapeutic exercise) may be important components of the overall therapeutic plan.

nursing_diagnosis.term: Risiko for uoppdaget kognitiv og atferdsmessig svikt (f.eks. eksekutiv dysfunksjon, språkvansker og apati) som kan skjules av, eller forveksles med, alvorlig motorisk funksjonsnedsettelse.

goal.term: Pasientens kognitive og atferdsmessige funksjon evalueres nøyaktig, uavhengig av graden av fysisk og kommunikativ funksjonsnedsettelse.

quotes: Cognitive impairment is an additional extra-motor feature of ALS that is highly prevalent, with some form of cognitive or behavioural symptoms experienced by 30–50% of people with ALS [ 9 ].

Cognitive impairment in people with ALS is characterised by executive dysfunction, impairments in language and social cognition [ 12 – 14 ], whilst apathy is the most pronounced behavioural change [ 15 ].

Of the eight trials that did assess cognitive and behaviour change as an outcome measure, six used tools where scoring was unaffected by physical disability or communication impairment and were specifically designed, and validated, to identify domains impaired in people with ALS.
One trial used the ALS Cognitive Behavioral Screen (ALS-CBS) [ 49 ] and five trials used the Edinburgh Cognitive and Behavioural ALS Screen (ECAS) [ 50 ].
Both are designed to be completed by either written or spoken responses to be suitable for ALS patients with differing functional ability.
Additionally, ECAS scoring is also corrected for differences in motor speed during both speech and writing tasks, to accommodate for a range in disability severity.

goal.term: Korrekt identifisering av angst og depresjon for å kunne iverksette treffsikre tiltak, uten at resultatene påvirkes av pasientens fysiske tilbakegang.

quotes: Background Up to 50% of people with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) experience cognitive dysfunction, whilst depression and anxiety are reported in up to 44% and 33%, respectively.
Symptoms of depression, anxiety and apathy reduce quality of life (QoL) for people with ALS [ 17 , 18 ].
Scores may be significantly impacted by the overlap of somatic features of neuropsychiatric conditions and those attributable to the progression of ALS.

This tool is validated to screen for depressive symptoms in people with ALS, with a sensitivity of 100% and specificity of 82%, meaning all people with a major depressive disorder were identified [ 36 ].
However, their reliance on somatic features of depression mean scores may be confounded by physical decline and no ALS-specific impairment thresholds are available.
The HADS is designed to reduce the focus on somatic symptoms and ALS-specific impairment thresholds have been proposed [ 44 ].

nursing_diagnosis.term: Risiko for mangelfull vurdering av selvmordsfare der normale pasientrefleksjoner rundt livets slutt kan feiltolkes som aktiv suicidalitet på grunn av den terminale diagnosen.

goal.term: Reell selvmordsfare avdekkes på en trygg måte, samtidig som pasientens naturlige refleksjoner rundt terminalfase og livets slutt respekteres og ikke patologiseres.

quotes: The C-SSRS is considered a useful tool to structure discussions of suicidal ideation and intent [ 45 ].

However, the C-SSRS can be lengthy to administer, insufficient to cover the full spectrum of suicidal ideation [ 46 ] and responses to questions of end-of-life planning may be influenced by the presence of a terminal diagnosis.

source_documents.title: SYMPTOMS, CLINICAL AND PHYSIOLOGICAL FINDINGS MOTIVATING HOME MECHANICAL VENTILATION IN PATIENTS WITH NEUROMUSCULAR DISEASES
source_documents.reasoning_trace: Dokumentet er en registerstudie som omhandler motiver for å starte mekanisk hjemmeventilasjon (HMV) hos pasienter med nevromuskulære sykdommer, deriblant ALS. For sykepleiere er det svært relevant å observere kliniske symptomer som dyspné, søvnighet på dagtid og hostesvikt, da disse indikerer behov for ventilasjonsstøtte. Videre fremhever dokumentet at ALS-pasienter kan oppleve rask sykdomsforverring (ofte trigget av infeksjoner) og at det er særskilte etiske aspekter knyttet til HMV (spesielt invasiv ventilasjon) for denne pasientgruppen. Ut fra dette har jeg formulert funn der sykepleierens rolle i administrasjon av HMV, symptomobservasjon og ivaretakelse av etiske hensyn ved rask progresjon, er i fokus. Målene er forankret i tekstens beskrivelse av at ventilasjonsstøtte gir symptomlindring og økt levetid.

source_documents.title: Can Therapeutic Exercise Slow Down Progressive Functional Decline in Patients With Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis? A Meta-Analysis
source_documents.reasoning_trace: Dokumentet omhandler effekten av terapeutisk trening for pasienter med amyotrofisk lateral sklerose (ALS). For sykepleiere på sykehus er det spesielt relevant å fokusere på pasientens evne til å mestre daglige aktiviteter (ADL), samt forebygging av respirasjonskomplikasjoner og sekundære inaktivitetskomplikasjoner. Jeg har identifisert tre sentrale sykepleiediagnoser med tilhørende intervensjoner og mål. Først tap av funksjon i daglige aktiviteter på grunn av muskelsvakhet, hvor veiledning i trening (styrke, motstand, aerob) er tiltaket, med mål om å opprettholde ADL-funksjon. Videre er risiko for respirasjonssvikt en alvorlig trussel ved ALS. Dokumentet fremhever at tidlig igangsetting av terapeutisk trening har en beskyttende effekt på lungefunksjonen. Til slutt er risikoen for inaktivitetsrelaterte komplikasjoner (dekondisjonering, osteoporose, inaktivitetsatrofi) vesentlig. Sykepleietiltaket innebærer å tilrettelegge for submaksimale aerobe øvelser og lett styrketrening tidlig i sykdomsforløpet for å forebygge dette.

source_documents.title: A systematic review of neuropsychiatric and cognitive assessments used in clinical trials for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
source_documents.reasoning_trace: Dokumentet er en systematisk gjennomgang av nevropsykiatriske og kognitive kartleggingsverktøy brukt i kliniske studier for ALS. Selv om dokumentet har et forskningsfokus, er kartlegging av kognitiv funksjon, depresjon, angst og selvmordsfare sentrale sykepleieoppgaver i en sykehusavdeling. Funnene er bygget på pasientens problemer (kognitiv svikt, depresjon, selvmordsfare) og intervensjonene retter seg mot å bruke verktøy som er spesifikt tilpasset pasientens fysiske og kommunikative begrensninger. Målene er utledet ved hjelp av klinisk skjønn, med fokus på at korrekt kartlegging skal sikre nøyaktig bilde av pasientens helse for å optimalisere livskvalitet og unngå at fysisk forfall (svekkelse) forveksles med psykiske symptomer.
